diagrams.description: Advertising is the practice of crafting and delivering persuasive messages to promote products, services, or ideas. This flowchart illustrates its systematic process: the ultimate goal is to build brand awareness, shape consumer attitudes, and drive purchasing behavior. It integrates core components—strategy and planning (defining objectives, target audience, budget, and creative brief), creative development (copywriting, art direction, visual/audio production, branding), and media planning and buying (selecting channels—TV, print, digital, social, outdoor; programmatic; scheduling). These are guided by theoretical foundations (AIDA, hierarchy of effects, elaboration likelihood model, persuasion principles) and unfold through a campaign cycle (research → strategy → creative → media → launch → evaluate → feedback). The resulting outcomes include brand recall, engagement, sales, and long‑term loyalty.
